jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 3+ years of non-internship design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience programming with at least one software programming language
- Experience in developing distributed software systems in C/C++, Rust, Golang or Python on Linux
- Experience with building large-scale, high-performance systems in a complex, multi-tiered, distributed environment
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon’s low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Key job responsibilities
- You will develop code, build CI/CD pipelines, test automation, and dashboards for monitoring health of systems and data pipelines.
- You will work with multiple cross functional teams to gather requirements, design, develop, deploy, and operate the infrastructure you build
- You will develop and mentor junior and new team members.
- You will engineer and build a cloud service that is reliable and available to provide connectivity for our customers
- You will work on features that enhance the quality of service and security for our customers.
- You will collaborate and work on initiatives across multiple teams to build networking functions on AWS cloud infrastructure and set best practices for software development processes

A day in the life
SDEs at Leo work on designing networking features with various stakeholders, implementing and testing them on prototype/production environments and delivering solutions with the highest quality. We write code, debug, and create the world’s most innovative products. We also operate our own service fleets and handle our customer issues to provide the best experience for our customers.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent degree in Business Administration, Finance, Engineering, or a related field and one year of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Must have one year of experience in the following skills: (1) direct management experience, including a salaried workforce, in a manufacturing, production or distribution environment; (2) experience using lean process improvement, six sigma methodologies or related performance metrics or process improvements to increase efficiency within processes, forecasting, planning, optimization, and logistics; (3) experience with the full staffing lifecycle, including interviewing, hiring, performance management, promotions and termination; and (4) experience conducting qualitative and quantitative data analysis to support business decision making and cross-functional initiatives.#0000
jobs.description:
MULTIPLE POSITIONS AVAILABLE

Employer: AMAZON.COM SERVICES LLC
Offered Position: Manager II, Operations
Job Location: Turlock, California
Job Number: AMZ9112370

Position Responsibilities:

Drive creation of quality initiatives, process change initiatives and other Lean initiatives to enable their functional area to meet and exceed business plan. Responsible for the overall safety, quality and performance and customer experience of Inbound and/or Outbound operations. Identify, design, develop, implement and execute new and existing processes, policies, goals, and solutions to reduce potential risks. Create schedules, identify and communicate risks to stakeholders, define risk mitigation strategies, and facilitate appropriate engineering solutions. Maintain metrics, reports, and process documentation to monitor and analyze delivery performance and key controls. Manage and lead a team of Operations and Area Managers to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives in their area, as well as providing needed support and resources for other staff initiatives. Coach and mentor the team to ensure performance objectives are met.

40 hours / week, 8:00am-5:00pm, Salary Range $70,800/year to $90,900/year.

Amazon is a total compensation company. Dependent on the position offered, equity, sign-on payments, and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits. For more information, visit:
https://www.aboutamazon.com/workplace/employee-benefits.

Amazon.com is an Equal Opportunity-Affirmative Action Employer – Minority / Female / Disability / Veteran / Gender Identity / Sexual Orientation.#0000

jobs.title: Manager II, Operations - AMZ9112370

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in supply chain management, operations, engineering, analytics or related field
- 5+ years of program or project management experience
- 5+ years of supply chain experience
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
- Knowledge of Microsoft Excel at an advanced level, including: pivot tables, macros, index/match, vlookup, VBA, data links, etc.
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Senior Supply Chain Manager to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will handle ambiguous initiatives independently, using domain expertise to define problems, determine goals, and design long-term solutions for our ultra-fast fulfillment network. You will work both tactically and strategically, driving innovation and solving complex problems in supply chain planning, forecasting, and network optimization.


Key job responsibilities
• Independently manage complex supply chain initiatives with considerable impact
• Design and deliver end-to-end solutions for complex supply chain challenges in our fast-paced fulfillment environment
• Present findings and recommendations to senior leadership, writing strategic narratives and communicating across diverse functions
• Develop and implement long-term supply chain strategies that balance speed, cost, and service performance
• Mitigate long-term risks and make strategic trade-offs between short and long-term needs
• Drive process improvements that impact long-term organizational goals across programs and potentially multiple countries
• Lead supply chain modeling and design projects from concept through implementation and scaling
• Partner with cross-functional teams including Operations, Finance, Product, Technology, and Science to optimize supply chain performance
• Manage forecasting, demand planning, and capacity planning to support rapid business growth
• Develop new processes and mechanisms for the organization while driving operational excellence
• Navigate and resolve contentious issues across stakeholder teams, building consensus and influencing without authority
• Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify root causes and implement scalable solutions
• Negotiate complex agreements and manage supplier relationships to reduce risk and optimize performance

About the team
The SSD, RSR+, and Perishables Supply Chain team manages end-to-end operations for Amazon's fastest, most complex fulfillment networks delivering fresh groceries and fast-moving general merchandise goods. We operate at the intersection of speed and complexity—balancing ultra-fast delivery commitments with the unique challenges of perishable inventory and high-velocity general merchandise.

The team is responsible for short-term capacity planning that ensures our facilities can handle daily volume fluctuations, inbound planning and design that optimizes how products flow into our network, forecasting that anticipates customer demand with precision, and in-stock management that keeps the right products available at the right time. This high-impact role owns strategic supply chain planning and design for a critical segment of Amazon's fastest-growing business. We're building the future of ultra-fast fulfillment.

jobs.title: Senior Supply Chain Manager, R2L Supply Chain

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of technical product or program management experience
- 7+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
- 3+ years of software development experience
- 5+ years of technical program management working directly with software engineering teams experience
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Senior Technical Program Manager for the Data Intelligence and Governance team. This role is at the heart of Amazon's foundational Security and Data Governance systems and is responsible for driving the delivery of highly scale AI solutions in the space.

The right candidate for this role is an experienced program manager with deep technical knowledge, business acumen, a security-first mindset, highly effective written and verbal communication. Characteristics that will make this candidate effective include demonstrated abilities to: [1] dive deep into complex problem spaces while maintaining focus on the larger strategic objectives, [2] build trust and negotiate mutually beneficial outcomes with partners through transparency and delivery on commitments, and [3] communicate with clarity and candor to distill complex topics into simple terms while practicing respect for diverse ideas.

Key job responsibilities
- You will partner with security and engineering leaders across Amazon business and security teams to work backwards from customer security outcomes, design initiatives to achieve those outcomes, and drive delivery against those commitments.
- You will operate amidst ambiguity, where the problem and strategy may not be defined. You will deliver clarity by leading through your team and collaborating with partner teams to define the strategic vision, and influence program and product roadmaps and strategies that will realize that vision.
- You will bring strong business and technical acumen to apply data-driven judgements and influence product, program, and architecture decisions.
- You will support Engineering teams in driving business and program reviews, aligning with stakeholders, and establishing mechanisms to continuously improve execution.
- You will be representing verbally and in writing complex decisions, trade-offs, and potential solutions clearly to senior security and business leaders.

About the team
About Amazon Security

Diverse Experiences
Amazon Security values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why Amazon Security?
At Amazon, security is central to maintaining customer trust and delivering delightful customer experiences. Our organization is responsible for creating and maintaining a high bar for security across all of Amazon’s products and services. We offer talented security professionals the chance to accelerate their careers with opportunities to build experience in a wide variety of areas including cloud, devices, retail, entertainment, healthcare, operations, and physical stores.

Inclusive Team Culture
In Amazon Security, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Ongoing DEI events and learning experiences inspire us to continue learning and to embrace our uniqueness. Addressing the toughest security challenges requires that we seek out and celebrate a diversity of ideas, perspectives, and voices.

Training & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, training, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

• Mitigate long-term risks and make strategic trade-offs between short and long-term needs
• Drive process improvements that impact long-term organizational goals across programs and potentially multiple countries
• Lead supply chain modeling and design projects from concept through implementation and scaling
• Partner with cross-functional teams including Operations, Finance, Product, Technology, and Science to optimize supply chain performance
• Manage forecasting, demand planning, and capacity planning to support rapid business growth
• Develop new processes and mechanisms for the organization while driving operational excellence
• Navigate and resolve contentious issues across stakeholder teams, building consensus and influencing without authority
• Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify root causes and implement scalable solutions
• Negotiate complex agreements and manage supplier relationships to reduce risk and optimize performance

About the team
The SSD, RSR+, and Perishables Supply Chain team manages end-to-end operations for Amazon's fastest, most complex fulfillment networks delivering fresh groceries and fast-moving general merchandise goods. We operate at the intersection of speed and complexity—balancing ultra-fast delivery commitments with the unique challenges of perishable inventory and high-velocity general merchandise.

The team is responsible for short-term capacity planning that ensures our facilities can handle daily volume fluctuations, inbound planning and design that optimizes how products flow into our network, forecasting that anticipates customer demand with precision, and in-stock management that keeps the right products available at the right time. This high-impact role owns strategic supply chain planning and design for a critical segment of Amazon's fastest-growing business. We're building the future of ultra-fast fulfillment.

jobs.basic_qualifications:

- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements

jobs.description:
At Amazon, we're working to be the most Customer-centric company on earth. One customer experience that we’re constantly looking to take to a new level is how we fulfill and deliver their orders. The goal of Amazon is to build a world class last mile operation. Amazon aims to exceed the expectations of our customers by ensuring that their orders, no matter how large or small, are delivered as quickly, accurately, and cost effectively as possible. To meet this goal, Amazon is continually striving to innovate and provide best in class service levels through the introduction of pioneering new products and services.

At Amazon we are building a world class last mile operation, significantly complimented by Delivery Service Partners (DSP). Amazon works directly with DSPs to make deliveries to our customers. As a DSP, these high-performing drivers can now be their own boss managing a fleet of up to 40 branded vans delivering Amazon packages.

We are looking for a Sr. Program Manager to join the Last Mile DSP Network Health Team. This person will be accountable for Director level goals and will need to work closely with senior leaders in the Last Mile space as well as key partner organizations to ensure success. The Sr. Program Manager will own building and reporting partner insights for Network Health (NH) and the Delivery Service Partners program. This includes working closely with legal, account managers, operations, tech and other internal teams to understand the business and refine models to more quickly identify risks in the DSP network.

You will be successful in this role if you 1) have a strong sense of ownership and seek long-term solutions that scale worldwide, 2) can develop data-based insights and turn them into actionable information, 3) have a desire to learn new areas of the business and able to apply those learnings to your own work and 4) clearly communicate, both verbally and in writing, to drive buy-in and adoption across a diverse group of stakeholders with competing priorities.

We are seeking a bar-raising candidate who will be able to:
• Develop scalable frameworks that will drive quality insights regarding DSP partners
• Build reporting mechanisms to identify trends in the network and enable Network Health and partner teams to make decisions quickly
• Partner with cross-functional stakeholders across various organizations, geographies, and leadership levels to drive programs that enable DSPs and Amazon Logistics to be successful
• Analyze metrics and patterns of behavior to inform business decisions
• Operate and deliver in a fast-paced environment

Key job responsibilities
• Define the strategic direction and build roadmap to develop against
• Build/design/refine frameworks to evaluate business relationships and identify varying risk within the DSP network
• Oversee the development and improvement of existing insight processes
• Integrate findings with partner team to influence Amazon Logistics and DSP engagement
• Communicate with senior management and other business/technical partners on program decisions, roadmap status and risks
• Collaborate with Data Analytics and Tech teams to articulate and prioritize feature needs for analytics and reporting
• Work in partnership with global partner teams to help drive worldwide parity across Network Health’s frameworks
• Create, maintain, and disseminate program information on a regular basis to senior leadership teams and key stakeholders.

jobs.description:
The Global Standards & Customer Excellence (GSCE) organization is seeking a Senior Process Improvement Specialist to drive network-wide projects and innovate new processes at scale to join our Cross Mile Continuous Improvement (XMCI) team.

The XMCI team is looking for a Senior Process Improvement Specialist who will serve as the execution engine of our network's continuous improvement pipeline — leading and facilitating kaizen events that deliver measurable, sustained results across NA Operations. Working from kaizen-ready charters developed by the XMCI scoping team, this role is responsible for planning, facilitating, and driving cross-functional kaizen events to successful completion — guiding diverse teams of stakeholders through structured problem-solving to implement solutions that improve safety, quality, people, and cost outcomes.

This role operates across multiple miles and business units within Amazon Stores, providing broad exposure to senior leadership up to the VP level and a unique vantage point into how Amazon's end-to-end logistics network operates.

This is a high-visibility, high-influence role that requires exceptional stakeholder management. You will lead kaizen events where the participants don't report to you — site General Managers, Operations Managers, Area Managers, process engineers, frontline associates, and support function leaders will all be in the room, and you will be the person responsible for aligning them around the problem, guiding them through root cause analysis and solution development, navigating competing priorities and perspectives, and driving the team to actionable outcomes within the event timeframe.

Your ability to build trust quickly, manage group dynamics, facilitate productive conflict, and hold teams accountable to the process will be the difference between a kaizen that delivers lasting change and one that produces a report that sits on a shelf. You will need to earn credibility with site leadership before, during, and after every event — demonstrating deep operational acumen, methodological rigor, and genuine respect for the expertise that local teams bring to the table.

Beyond facilitation, you will own the end-to-end event lifecycle — from pre-event planning through post-event follow-up and sustainment handoff. You will partner upstream with the XMCI scoping team to review charters, validate readiness, and ensure you have the right team composition and stakeholder alignment before an event begins. You will lead the event itself — structuring the agenda, teaching and applying Lean tools and methodologies, guiding the team of multi-business unit leaders up to the VP level through current state validation, root cause analysis, solution development, and implementation planning. And you will own the post-event close-out — documenting results, developing sustainment plans with site leadership, and handing off validated outcomes to the XMCI analytics team for financial validation and entitlement tracking. You will also provide regular reporting on event execution, results, and sustainment status to XMCI leadership and network stakeholders.

Throughout all of this, you will serve as a brand ambassador for XMCI and a champion of Amazon's culture of Lean Excellence — modeling what world-class facilitation looks like, building local CI capability through every event you lead, and empowering site leadership to own and sustain the improvements long after the event is complete.

The right candidate is an experienced kaizen facilitator who thrives in front of a room, builds trust quickly across all levels of an organization, and is energized by the challenge of guiding diverse teams to breakthrough results in compressed timeframes.

This role will require travel up to 50% of the time.


Key job responsibilities
Event Planning & Stakeholder Alignment
• Partner with the XMCI scoping team to review kaizen-ready charters, validate event readiness, and ensure the problem statement, scope, baseline metrics, and business case are sound before committing to execution
• Lead pre-event stakeholder engagement — aligning site leadership, process owners, and functional partners on event objectives, expectations, roles, and commitments well in advance of the event
• Develop detailed event plans including agenda design, Lean tool selection, data requirements, logistics, and pre-work assignments tailored to the specific problem and site context
• Assemble and confirm the right cross-functional team composition for each event — ensuring the right mix of decision-makers, subject matter experts, process owners, and frontline associates are in the room
• Build trust and credibility with site leadership ahead of each event — demonstrating respect for local expertise, understanding of site-specific context, and clarity on what the event will and will not accomplish
• Manage stakeholder expectations across multiple levels — from frontline participants to site GMs to senior leaders up to the VP level — ensuring alignment on objectives, timeline, and anticipated outcomes
Kaizen Event Facilitation & Execution
• Lead and facilitate kaizen events across multiple miles and business units within Amazon Stores — guiding cross-functional teams through structured problem-solving methodologies to deliver measurable improvements in safety, quality, people, and cost
• Structure and manage the event agenda — teaching and applying the appropriate Lean tools and methodologies (value stream mapping, root cause analysis, 5 Why, fishbone diagrams, standard work, process flow analysis, waste walks, spaghetti diagrams, PDCA, and others) based on the nature of the problem
• Guide teams through current state validation — leveraging the baseline data and root cause hypotheses from the charter while encouraging the team to verify and build on those findings through direct observation and analysis during the event
• Facilitate productive dialogue, healthy conflict, and consensus-building among diverse stakeholders with competing priorities and perspectives — navigating group dynamics with confidence, empathy, and methodological discipline
• Drive the team from problem understanding through solution development and implementation planning within the event timeframe — maintaining pace, focus, and energy while ensuring rigor is not sacrificed for speed
• Lead the development of actionable implementation plans with clear owners, timelines, and success metrics — ensuring solutions are practical, sustainable, and owned by the people who will live with them after the event
• Manage real-time event risks — adjusting scope, approach, or team dynamics as needed to keep the event on track and productive
• Ensure all events are executed in alignment with XMCI methodology standards and reflect Amazon's commitment to Lean Excellence
Post-Event Close-Out & Sustainment
• Own the post-event close-out process — documenting event results, solutions implemented, projected financial and operational impact, and open action items in a clear, standardized format
• Develop comprehensive sustainment plans in partnership with site leadership — defining who owns each improvement, how it will be monitored, what metrics will be tracked, and what triggers should prompt escalation if results begin to erode
• Facilitate event report-outs to site leadership, XMCI leadership, and senior stakeholders — clearly communicating what was accomplished, what the projected impact is, and what is needed to sustain the results
• Hand off validated event results and documentation to the XMCI analytics team for financial validation and entitlement tracking — ensuring clean, complete, and Finance-ready data
• Conduct post-event follow-ups at defined intervals (30/60/90 days) to verify sustainment, support site leadership in maintaining momentum, and identify any corrective actions needed
• Provide regular reporting to XMCI leadership and network stakeholders on event execution status, results delivered, sustainment health, and lessons learned
Stakeholder Management & Relationship Building
• Build strong, trust-based relationships with site General Managers, Operations Managers, Area Managers, and functional leaders across multiple miles and business units within NA Operations — earning the credibility needed to lead effective events in environments where you have no direct authority
• Influence without authority at every level — from frontline associates to VPs — navigating the matrixed, cross-business unit environment with sound judgment, empathy, and a bias for action
• Serve as a communications consultant and brand ambassador for XMCI — ensuring every event and stakeholder interaction reinforces the team's reputation for world-class facilitation, operational rigor, and genuine partnership
• Manage the delicate balance between driving results during the event and respecting the operational realities and constraints of the host site — earning repeat invitations by being the kind of facilitator that site leadership wants to work with again
• Build local CI capability through every event — teaching participants the tools and thinking, not just leading them through the motions, so that site teams are more capable of driving improvement on their own after the event
• Gather and incorporate stakeholder feedback after every event to continuously improve the facilitation experience and strengthen the XMCI brand
Methodology, Standards & Continuous Improvement
• Develop and continuously refine standardized facilitation guides, event templates, tools, and playbooks that ensure consistency and quality across all XMCI kaizen events regardless of site, mile, or business unit
• Contribute to the evolution of XMCI's continuous improvement operating system — providing field-level insights from event execution that inform how charters are scoped, how events are designed, and how sustainment is structured
• Partner with the XMCI scoping team to provide feedback on charter quality — creating a feedback loop that strengthens both the scoping and facilitation functions over time
• Partner with the XMCI analytics team to ensure event documentation and results capture are structured for clean financial validation and entitlement tracking from the outset
• Stay current on industry best practices in Lean, Six Sigma, facilitation, and change management — bringing outside-in thinking to Amazon's CI ecosystem and continuously raising the bar on what world-class kaizen facilitation looks like
• Champion a culture of continuous improvement that empowers local leadership to own and sustain results — reinforcing XMCI's dual focus on operational optimization and building a common Amazon CI culture

A day in the life
- Act as a strategic advisor to process improvement managers and field operators, providing guidance on developing and executing effective network-level program expansion, or process changes.
- Develop and maintain schedules, ensuring timely and proactive dissemination of curriculum to stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle.


About the team
The XMCI team's mission is to be the standard for world-class continuous improvement for global logistics to support Amazon's goal of becoming Earth's Best Employer. We work backwards from customer needs to develop mechanisms, technology, and content to deliver the right training at the right time. These training solutions prepare our learners to be successful in driving safety, quality, people and cost metrics within our business.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree or above in engineering, project management, operations, logistics, supply chain or related field
- Experience Experienced with end-to-end ownership of major project deliverables
- Experience creating reports, interpreting metrics and driving overall insights or analysis

jobs.description:
The Global Standards & Customer Excellence (GSCE) organization is seeking a Senior Process Improvement Specialist to drive network-wide projects and innovate new processes at scale to join our Cross Mile Continuous Improvement (XMCI) team.

The XMCI team is seeking a Senior Process Improvement Specialist with strong analyst skills to own the metrics framework, financial validation, entitlement tracking, and reporting narrative that tells the story of XMCI's impact across NA Operations. This is a process improvement role with a data-driven focus — you won't be building the data infrastructure yourself, but you will be the CI and business subject matter expert who defines what needs to be measured, why it matters, and how it should be presented to leadership. You will partner closely with Business Intelligence and Engineering (BIE) resources to translate your requirements into the dashboards, data products, and automated solutions that power XMCI's reporting ecosystem. This role operates across multiple miles and business units within Amazon Stores, providing broad exposure to senior leadership up to the VP level and a unique vantage point into how Amazon's end-to-end logistics network operates.

Your most visible responsibility will be creating and maintaining the team's primary reporting artifacts — the documents and narratives included in all business reviews across the organization. You will own the metrics framework, business logic, and executive storytelling that provide XMCI leadership, NA Operations stakeholders, and senior executives with a clear, accurate, and compelling picture of our CI program's performance and financial health. You will define the reporting requirements and work with BIE partners to build and refine the dashboards and data products that bring those requirements to life. You will be responsible for ensuring our reporting is business review-ready at all times — current, validated, defensible, and actionable — and your ownership of this ecosystem will directly shape how leadership perceives and invests in the CI program.

Underpinning the reporting is the analytical and governance work you will own. You will define what data needs to be pulled from across the XMCI ecosystem — kaizen charters, event results, Finance validation outputs, entitlement tracking, and network operational metrics — and partner with BIE resources to build the pipelines and models that aggregate it into a holistic view of program performance. You will own the end-to-end financial validation lifecycle, partnering with Finance business partners to substantiate and gain formal sign-off on all savings claims. You will design entitlement tracking mechanisms that monitor benefit sustainment over time, flagging at-risk entitlements and feeding decay pattern insights back to the team. And you will define the standardized metrics, savings categorizations, and Finance-approved calculation methodologies that create consistency and auditability across the entire XMCI program.

The right candidate is a process improvement professional with the business analyst mindset to define what needs to be measured and the CI expertise to know why it matters. You are comfortable working with data — proficient in Excel, able to navigate data sets, and fluent in translating business needs into clear requirements for technical partners. You are obsessive about data quality and accuracy. But above all, you understand that data without narrative is just noise — and you have the communication skills to turn numbers into the story that earns trust, drives decisions, and proves the value of Lean Excellence at Amazon scale.


Key job responsibilities
Key Responsibilities
Business Review Reporting & Program Analytics
• Own the creation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of XMCI's primary reporting artifacts — including the documents, narratives, and data products included in all WBRs, MBRs, QBRs, and senior leadership business reviews
• Own the metrics framework, business logic, and executive narratives that drive the team's reporting — defining what needs to be measured, how it should be calculated, and how the story should be told to leadership audiences up to the VP level
• Partner with BIE resources to translate reporting requirements into dashboards, automated data products, and visualization tools — serving as the business owner who defines the "what" and "why" while BIE partners build the "how"
• Ensure all team reporting is business review-ready at all times with current, validated, and defensible data, clear narratives, and actionable insights that communicate XMCI's program performance and financial impact
• Proactively identify trends, anomalies, and insights in program data that surface actionable recommendations on where XMCI is delivering, where it's falling short, and where to allocate resources
• Produce ad hoc deep dives, variance analyses, and supplemental reporting packages as requested by XMCI leadership, NA Operations stakeholders, or senior executives
• Serve as the single point of accountability for XMCI reporting quality — if a number appears in a business review, you have verified it and can defend it
Financial Validation & Entitlement Tracking
• Own the end-to-end financial validation process for XMCI kaizen events — partnering with Finance business partners to review, substantiate, and gain formal sign-off on all savings and cost avoidance claims
• Design and maintain entitlement tracking mechanisms that monitor benefit sustainment at 30/60/90 days and beyond, incorporating realization data into the broader reporting ecosystem
• Reconcile CI-reported savings with Finance-recognized savings, resolving discrepancies with transparency and analytical rigor
• Flag at-risk entitlements, track benefit decay patterns across the network, and surface sustainment insights to XMCI leadership, charter developers, and kaizen facilitators
• Maintain a centralized, auditable financial portfolio of all validated savings, tracked entitlements, and benefit realization status across the XMCI kaizen program
Data Requirements & BIE Partnership
• Define the data requirements, business logic, and source system needs that underpin XMCI's reporting and tracking ecosystem — translating CI and Finance domain knowledge into clear, actionable specifications for BIE partners
• Partner with BIE resources to build, test, and refine the dashboards, data pipelines, and automated reporting workflows that power the team's analytics capabilities
• Serve as the business owner and subject matter expert for all XMCI data products — owning the requirements backlog, prioritizing BIE development work based on business impact, and validating outputs against CI and Finance standards
• Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting and tracking processes through partnership with BIE — reducing manual effort while increasing data freshness, accuracy, and scalability
• Maintain data integrity and quality standards across all reporting outputs — proactively identifying and resolving data gaps, inconsistencies, and errors before they reach a business review audience
Governance, Standards & Methodology
• Define and maintain the standardized metrics definitions, savings categorizations (hard, soft, cost avoidance, productivity), and calculation methodologies used across the entire XMCI team
• Develop and maintain a benefits calculation guide that provides kaizen teams with Finance-approved formulas and structures for projecting savings during charter development
• Conduct periodic calibration sessions with Finance partners to ensure ongoing alignment on definitions, recognition criteria, and reporting standards
• Ensure all reporting and tracking mechanisms comply with Amazon Finance policies and are audit-ready at all times
• Continuously improve the XMCI reporting and governance ecosystem — identifying process gaps, refining standards, and raising the bar on data quality, timeliness, and analytical depth
Stakeholder Partnership & Communication
• Serve as XMCI's primary metrics and analytics liaison to Finance, Operations, and senior leadership — translating complex data into clear, compelling narratives tailored to each audience
• Partner upstream with charter developers and midstream with kaizen facilitators to ensure financial measurement plans are structured for clean data capture and validation-ready reporting from the outset
• Build trust-based relationships with Finance business partners to ensure CI savings validation is a collaborative, efficient process
• Build and maintain a productive working relationship with BIE resources — clearly communicating requirements, providing timely feedback, and ensuring technical solutions align with business needs and CI domain standards
- Up to 50% travel required.

A day in the life
- Act as a strategic advisor to process improvement managers and field operators, providing guidance on developing and executing effective network-level program expansion, or process changes.
- Develop and maintain schedules, ensuring timely and proactive dissemination of curriculum to stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle.


About the team
The XMCI team's mission is to be the standard for world-class continuous improvement for global logistics to support Amazon's goal of becoming Earth's Best Employer. We work backwards from customer needs to develop mechanisms, technology, and content to deliver the right training at the right time. These training solutions prepare our learners to be successful in driving safety, quality, people and cost metrics within our business.

jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Knowledge of Lean, Six Sigma and Kaizen techniques
- Experience identifying and aggregating trends, past performance metrics, and insights into effective narratives, with recommendations, to external senior leaders

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. Your Amazon package will include sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon also offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, Basic Life & AD&D insurance and option for Supplemental life plans, EAP, Mental Health Support, Medical Advice Line, Flexible Spending Accounts, Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement coverage), 401(k) matching, paid time off, and parental leave. Learn more about our benefits at https://amazon.jobs/en/benefits.
